finding: Raw WebDriver BiDi server frames require a staged, fail-closed evidence path: transport classification must succeed before semantic deployment verification, and rejection must preserve the failing stage rather than flattening protocol and claim failures into one result.
design_implication: Browser adapters can call one pipeline that preserves wire-stage protocol errors, semantic-stage contract failures, accepted frame counts, schemas, and trust limits.

cases: valid raw frames pass both stages
invalid JSON fails at wire stage
runtime commit mismatch fails at semantic stage
client command on server stream fails before semantics

uncertainty: No live browser WebSocket transcript was captured.
The WebDriver BiDi specification remains a Working Draft and may change.
Pipeline acceptance is not cryptographic provenance, browser conformance, physical output proof, or user-perception proof.
falsification_route: Capture server-to-client frames from a current BiDi implementation, replay them through verifyBidiEvidenceFrames, and reject or revise the contract if valid implementation output is misclassified or if invalid output reaches semantic acceptance.
